title: salix nummularia var. cyclophylla
intro: Salix nummularia var. cyclophylla, also known as the Round-leaf Willow, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to Australia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, derived from its bark and leaves. The Round-leaf Willow has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of Salix nummularia var. cyclophylla include infusions, decoctions, and topical applications, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, from minor cuts and scrapes to more severe conditions.
